searchProcess.robotsOrAccessNotes: med.stanford.edu accessible. International IVS lane is well-documented with verbatim eligibility, fees, duration, visa-responsibility language. TOEFL/IELTS verbatim retrieval queued to ivs-admission-requirements sub-page in a future packet retry.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Visiting Student Program | Stanford Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: Students applying to participate in the IVS Program must be in their final year of medical school. To be eligible for the IVS Program, you must currently be enrolled in a medical school that is part of a university.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Visiting Student Program | Stanford Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: If you already hold an MD degree or its equivalent, you are not eligible to apply to the IVS Program.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Visiting Student Program | Stanford Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: A non-refundable Application Fee of $300 US Dollars is due via SlideRoom. $3,100 US Dollars for a 2 week elective. $6,200 US Dollars for a 4 week elective. $250 US Dollars Document Fee.

candidateFindings.pageTitle: International Visiting Student Program | Stanford Medicine
candidateFindings.shortQuote: IVS Program participants may require visa for entry into the United States... are the student's responsibility.

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: IMG graduates (post-MD) explicitly excluded from IVS Program; observership-for-graduates lane not surfaced on Stanford's visiting-clerkships hub.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Stanford Hospital / Stanford Health Care not separately named as placement site on the IVS hub (clerkships are department-organized); TOEFL/IELTS verbatim retrieval queued to ivs-admission-requirements sub-page.

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) INSTITUTION_SPECIFIC source — Stanford SOM IVS hub; (b) final-year MS in university-based MS only; (c) MD graduates explicitly EXCLUDED (no observership-for-grads lane here); (d) $300 app fee via SlideRoom (NOT VSLO for IVS lane); (e) $3,100/2wk, $6,200/4wk, $12,400 max for two 4wk; (f) $250 one-time document fee; (g) no housing provided; (h) visa is student responsibility, no Stanford support; (i) max 8 weeks. Manual retry queued to capture TOEFL/IELTS verbatim from ivs-admission-requirements sub-page.
plainEnglishSummary: Stanford runs a real International Visiting Student Program for final-year medical students at university-based medical schools. MD graduates and post-graduate IMG observers are explicitly excluded. Fees are stiff: $300 application + $250 document + $3,100 for a 2-week elective or $6,200 for a 4-week elective, max two 4-week electives ($12,400 total). No housing. Visa is the student's responsibility. Application goes through SlideRoom (not VSLO for the international lane).
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because Stanford IVS lane was captured verbatim with the most detailed cost breakdown of any P101 finding so far; also captured an important MD-graduate exclusion that means no IMG-friendly claim is permitted on this listing.
